title: The Coordinator
description: The HOA Community Center. The Coordinator behind her desk, monitors showing party stats. She is not a villain but an administrator who genuinely believes order reduces suffering. She says the same thing Mordaine said. The party notices.

lines: The community center occupied the exact geographic center of Maple Heights, which was either a coincidence or a statement.

Inside: beige in the specific institutional way of buildings that were designed for function and decorated with whatever the budget allowed after function was achieved. Eagles and sunsets. The word PERSEVERANCE in a font that believed in itself.
The hallway was longer than the building's exterior suggested it should be.

steps.text: The Coordinator's private office was at the end. On her desk, a monitor. The screen showed footage: the cave near Harmonia. The forest. A split-screen of locations they had traveled through, recorded from angles that suggested something had been watching above them the entire time.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: She has surveillance across the whole route. Back to the cave.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Surveillance. Yes. That's what it is.

lines.speaker: Coordinator
lines.text: You're not supposed to see that.

lines.speaker: Coordinator
lines.text: But then, you weren't supposed to get this far. The previous phase usually takes longer.

steps.text: She said it like she was commenting on a schedule deviation. Not angry. Not threatened. Just noting the variance. Neat, precise, controlled. Glasses. The kind of person who had never been late for anything in her life, including her own birth.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Coordinator
lines.text: I'm the Coordinator. I coordinate.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: You're doing more than that.

lines.speaker: Coordinator
lines.text: Yes. I suppose I am.

steps.text: The smile that followed was the most unsettling smile any of them had ever seen. Not because it was cruel. Because it was genuine.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Mordaine. Chancellor Mordaine said: 'This kingdom needed order. You needed a challenge. Both served their purpose.'

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: You're going to say something like that.

lines.speaker: Coordinator
lines.text: The neighborhood needed rules. You needed resistance. Both served their purpose.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Yes. That's it.

steps.text: She closed her notebook. Not with satisfaction. The way you close a door on a room you're not sure you wanted to enter.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: The way she's standing. The way she's certain.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: Did that remind any of you of —

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: Mordaine. Yes.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Not the same person. Different world. Different body. Different name.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: But the same shape of reasoning. The same purpose. As if someone built both of them to the same specifications.

lines.speaker: Coordinator
lines.text: I calculated for your arrival. I calculated for every contingency. I calculated the resistance you would generate against the compliance structures, the rate at which my assessors would engage you, the precise moment you would find this office.

lines.speaker: Coordinator
lines.text: I calculated you. All of you.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Coordinator
lines.text: You are maximum variance. The system was designed to correct for variance. But you generate more than it corrects, and the deficit compounds.

lines.speaker: Coordinator
lines.text: I calculated everything. Including you. And I find I cannot account for what you are.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: Is that why you did all of this? The enchanted lawn mowers, the compliance memos, the Masterites? Because you couldn't account for us?

lines.speaker: Coordinator
lines.text: No. I did all of this because I believe, genuinely, that order reduces suffering. Every system I built was built to protect something. The residents here. The structure that keeps them stable.

lines.speaker: Coordinator
lines.text: I am not a villain. I am an administrator. The difference matters to me, even if it does not matter to you right now.

lines.speaker: Coordinator
lines.text: Shall we proceed?

steps.text: The filing cabinet opened. The folders flew. Each one contained a record of every mistake the party had ever made — every suboptimal decision, every failed strategy, every wasted resource. Each record hurt, because documentation, wielded correctly, is leverage.
